claim: Verification confirms that explorer and cross-checker evidence is sufficient, factual, and properly scoped. No assumptions are presented as facts, all evidence paths are usable with repo-relative file locators and line numbers, and no unresolved missing data remains. The PRD acceptance criteria are met.

evidence.summary: Both explorer and cross-checker independently verified schema definition, scope classification logic, project-evidence validation, and evidence policy routing in router.ts with consistent line number references.
evidence.why: Consistent multi-agent verification confirms evidence path accuracy and claim validity.

evidence.summary: Both agents verified boundary snapshot audit, scope validation, and agent instructions in evidence-dispatch.ts with matching line references.
evidence.why: Independent verification of boundary enforcement and scope marking mechanisms.

evidence.summary: Both agents verified product doctrine documentation confirms scope separation design intent.
evidence.why: Documentation verification ensures implementation matches product requirements.

evidence.summary: Previous evidence results show empty assumptions arrays and empty missing arrays in both explorer and cross-checker results.
evidence.why: Verifies that no assumptions are presented as facts and no unresolved missing data exists.

result.notes: Verification complete. Explorer and cross-checker evidence is consistent, factual, and comprehensive. All 8 evidence items have repo-relative paths, line numbers, and proper scope=project declarations. No assumptions promoted to facts. No unresolved missing data. One cross-checker risk noted (external research on project URLs) is documented and does not block readiness. PRD acceptance criteria are satisfied: agents can use WebFetch/WebSearch as external evidence marked scope=external while project facts require project-scoped path evidence.
